seoTitle: North Moonta vs North Plympton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between North Moonta and North Plympton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: North Moonta vs North Plympton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in North Moonta and North Plympton.
heading: North Moonta vs North Plympton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between North Moonta, South Australia and North Plympton, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: North Plympton has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to North Moonta (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is North Moonta or North Plympton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: North Moonta has a flood risk score of 43/100, while North Plympton scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: North Moonta scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. North Plympton scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in North Moonta or North Plympton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. North Plympton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
